nodes.context: Large Catholic sectarian street gang active in Glasgow, one of the Glasgow razor gangs, active from 1880s to 1960s, based in Norman Street, Bridgeton.
Protestant street gang in Bridgeton, Glasgow, rivals of the Norman Conks.
Mostly Catholic street gang in Glasgow, on friendly terms with the Norman Conks.

edges.evidence_quote: They were often involved in street fighting with the Protestant Billy Boys of Bridgeton
edges.context: The Norman Conks and Billy Boys were engaged in street fighting, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: but were on friendlier terms with the Calton Tongs who were also mostly Catholic
edges.context: The Norman Conks had friendly relations with the Calton Tongs, indicating cooperation.
